capabilities: Implement a Pratt parser to efficiently handle operator precedence in expression parsing by utilizing prefix and infix parselets with specific binding powers.

procedure.action: Initialize the parser by setting up a token stream and defining a function to retrieve the next token, ensuring the stream correctly handles end-of-input scenarios using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Specify prefix parselets by associating each prefix operator with a parselet function that processes the token and returns a partial abstract syntax tree (AST) node by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Specify infix parselets by associating each infix operator with a parselet function that processes the left-hand side expression and returns a combined AST node, using binding power to determine precedence by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Parse expressions by recursively invoking parselet functions based on the current token's type and binding power, ensuring correct precedence and associativity using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Construct the final abstract syntax tree by repeatedly parsing expressions until the token stream is exhausted, verifying that all operators and operands are correctly represented using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Implement a Pratt parser to manage operator precedence using prefix and infix parselets with binding power for efficient expression parsing.
title: Pratt Parser for Operator Precedence

failureConditions: Incorrect operator precedence when parselets are not assigned appropriate binding powers, causing misinterpretation of expressions.
Incomplete AST construction when the parser fails to handle all token types, leading to syntax errors or incomplete parsing.
Infinite recursion when parselet functions do not correctly handle base cases, causing stack overflow and parser failure.
metrics: all infix operators respect defined precedence levels
successConditions: binding_power_correctness: assert all infix operators respect defined precedence levels
